beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, and a quick check of the morning weather and news.
beats.reasoning: Ethan starts his Friday at home in Eastlake with his usual morning routine. Yesterday's energy was 50/100 and settled—he's carrying that calm into today but the warm weather and Pride festivities ahead have him mildly energized.

beats.activity: Commute to Ballard office via back roads to avoid any early Pride-related traffic.
beats.reasoning: Ethan prefers quieter routes and will anticipate the evening Capitol Hill closures. Leaving early gives him a smooth 40-minute commute through less congested streets.

beats.activity: Deep focus on Q3 dashboard refinements and morning standup; office is quiet and productive.
beats.reasoning: Friday mornings are typically calm before the weekend push. Ethan thrives in quiet focus time and will use the morning to push through analysis work before the day warms up.

beats.activity: Lunch break: walk to a local bagel spot in Ballard (strong opinions about bagels make this a ritual).
beats.reasoning: Ethan has strong bagel opinions and uses lunch as a chance to get outside on this warm day. A nearby Ballard spot keeps the break short and lets him enjoy the 77F weather.
Short walk back to the office to resume afternoon work.

beats.activity: Afternoon work: prep for stakeholder meetings and finalize weekly reports.
beats.reasoning: Ethan finishes out his work week with meetings and report polishing. He'll aim to leave by 5pm to avoid the worst of the evening rush and Capitol Hill closures.

beats.activity: Commute home to Eastlake, taking side streets to avoid Broadway/Pike closures around Capitol Hill.
beats.reasoning: Ethan leaves work early enough to sidestep the evening Pride street fair chaos. He'll take a deliberate 50-minute route home via I-5 or side roads, avoiding downtown entirely.

beats.activity: Home wind-down: change into casual clothes, pour a drink, spin a vinyl record.
beats.reasoning: Ethan decompresses at home with his vinyl collection. The warm evening and Pride festivities are happening elsewhere; he values quiet time with close friends or solitude over big crowds, so he's choosing to stay in his neighborhood.

beats.activity: Evening walk around Eastlake pocket parks and waterfront, birdwatching as the light softens.
beats.reasoning: The warm, breezy weather and golden hour are perfect for a local walk and birdwatching. Ethan stays in his home neighborhood rather than venture into the crowded Pride festivities downtown.

beats.activity: Prepare and enjoy a simple dinner at home; text Marcus about weekend plans.
beats.reasoning: Ethan returns home to cook a simple meal and catch up with close friends via text. He's winding down for the evening and staying true to his preference for intimate time over crowd scenes.

beats.activity: Evening leisure: reading or browsing vinyl collection, unwinding before bed.
beats.reasoning: Ethan spends his final waking hours at home in a calm, grounded state. He's satisfied with a quiet Friday focused on work, nature, and personal time.
Ethan heads to bed for a solid 8-hour sleep, ending well before 06:00 as required. The beat spans overnight into Saturday morning.

world_event_prompt: Warm, breezy Friday, high of 77F. Seattle Pride kickoff: Capitol Hill street fair and road closures along Broadway and Pike from 5pm. Festive, busy evening downtown.
